tool_args.label: Jaibala Patel, Chief Financial Officer

tool_args.label: Subway resumes 24-hour service
tool_args.raw_text: On May 17th, the subway returned to 24 hour, 7 day a week service for our customers

payload.description: The New York City Subway resumed 24 hour a day service on May 17

tool_args.label: 181st Street Busway Launch
tool_args.raw_text: I was excited to join Mayor de Blasio, Chairman Rodriguez and our partner at NYC DOT, Commissioner Gutman to celebrate the launch of the 181st Street Busway

payload.description: Launch of the 181st Street Busway

tool_args.label: City announces 28 miles of bus lanes/busways for 2021
tool_args.raw_text: On May 11th, the City announced plans to nearly double last year's record bus project totals. The administration plans to install 28 miles of new and improved busways and bus lanes in 2021.

payload.description: City announced plans to install 28 miles of new busways and bus lanes in 2021

tool_args.label: 181st Street Busway
tool_args.raw_text: the launch of the 181st Street Busway, another great milestone for NYC bus customers as we strive to deliver faster and more efficient bus service

payload.description: Busway launched in May 2021 with preliminary speed increases of up to 34%

tool_args.label: Zoning for Accessibility joint initiative announced
tool_args.raw_text: MTA, NYC DCP, & MOPD announced a new joint initiative called Zoning for Accessibility, a proposed change to the City's zoning resolution that would engage private developers in investing in subway accessibility improvements.

payload.description: MTA, NYC DCP and MOPD announced Zoning for Accessibility joint initiative

tool_args.label: Pandemic-era subway ridership record April 30
tool_args.raw_text: Average weekday ridership in April approached 2 million and reached a new pandemic-era record of 2.162 million on April 30th.

payload.description: New pandemic-era record subway ridership on April 30, 2021

tool_args.label: Highest single day bus ridership since front-door boarding resumed
tool_args.raw_text: on May 6th, we reached the highest single day ridership since front-door boarding resumed last summer, with approximately 1.26 million riders—over 53% of our pre-COVID ridership levels.

payload.description: Highest single day bus ridership since front-door boarding resumed

tool_args.label: Preliminary speed increases on 181st Street Busway
tool_args.raw_text: Since the launch of this busway we are already seeing preliminary speed increases of up to 34%.

payload.description: Preliminary speed increases on the 181st Street Busway since launch

tool_args.raw_text: the launch of the 181st Street Busway, another great milestone for NYC bus customers

payload.description: Busway corridor launched in May 2021 with preliminary speed increases of up to 34%

tool_args.label: City plans 28 miles of bus lanes and busways in 2021
tool_args.raw_text: The administration plans to install 28 miles of new and improved busways and bus lanes in 2021. These projects include completion of five busways, 11.5 miles of new bus lanes, 16.3 miles of improved lanes, as well as other bus priority enhancements such as new bus boarding islands, street redesign and transit signal priority.

payload.claim_text: The administration plans to install 28 miles of new and improved busways and bus lanes in 2021 including completion of five busways, 11.5 miles of new bus lanes, 16.3 miles of improved lanes, as well as new bus boarding islands, street redesign and transit signal priority.

tool_args.label: Paratransit broker car service contracts for $655M
tool_args.raw_text: This month, NYCT is seeking Board approval to award four, 3-year estimated quantity service contracts for the provision of Broker Car Service for Paratransit customers. The contracts have an aggregate estimated amount of $655 million

payload.claim_text: NYCT is seeking Board approval to award four 3-year estimated quantity service contracts for Broker Car Service for Paratransit customers with aggregate estimated amount of $655 million.

tool_args.label: Paratransit ridership at 80% of pre-pandemic levels
tool_args.raw_text: Paratransit ridership continues to recover at a faster pace than all other MTA agencies with weekday ridership reaching a high of 80% of pre-pandemic levels.

payload.claim_text: Paratransit weekday ridership reached a high of 80% of pre-pandemic levels.

payload.description: Subway weekday customer journey time performance April 2021
Bus service delivered (peak hours) April 2021 systemwide

tool_args.label: New accessible entrance opened at Court Sq-23 St station
tool_args.raw_text: A new accessible entrance to the Manhattan-bound E and M train platform of the Court Sq-23 St station opened this spring.

payload.description: New accessible entrance to the Manhattan-bound E and M train platform of Court Sq-23 St station
payload.event_name: Court Sq-23 St accessible entrance opening

tool_args.label: Tactile and Braille map installed at 23 St station
tool_args.raw_text: Last month, we installed a new tactile and Braille map of the 1 line at the 23 St station in Manhattan.

payload.description: Installation of tactile and Braille map of the 1 line at 23 St station
payload.event_name: Tactile Braille map installation at 23 St

tool_args.label: Pop-up vaccination centers launched in 8 MTA stations
tool_args.raw_text: The walk-up centers, established in eight MTA stations – E 180 St, Jamaica-179 St, Coney Island-Stillwell Av, Broadway Junction, Penn Station (34 St Corridor), Grand Central Terminal (Vanderbilt Hall), Hempstead Terminal (LIRR), and Ossining Station (Metro-North) – vaccinated 1,112 people on its first day

payload.description: Pop-up vaccination centers launched in eight MTA stations

tool_args.raw_text: April 2021 farebox revenue of $168.7 million was $69.8 million (70.5 percent) above budget.

tool_args.label: Major felonies Jan-Apr 2021 at 446

tool_args.label: Subway fires increased to 960 in 12-month period
tool_args.raw_text: Subway Fires 2 769 757 960

payload.claim_text: Subway fires increased to 960 in the 12-month period May 2020-April 2021, compared to 757 in the prior 12-month period and 769 two years prior.
